channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Elon Musk’s near-daily online posts about race are turning off some fans
item.description: The billionaire posted 850 times on the topic in recent months, almost triple the rate for the previous two years.

item.title: Camp Mystic, where 27 people died in floods, could reopen despite notice that its license is imperiled
item.description: Texas health officials notified the owners of Camp Mystic that their pending license renewal may be denied because the camp has not met health and safety requirements. The camp says it will address the deficiencies.

item.title: Iran talks to resume this weekend without Vance
item.description: The White House will send Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, but not Vice President JD Vance, to Pakistan as Tehran decides not to send its top negotiator.

item.title: Appeals court clears way for U.S. to reopen border for asylum seekers
item.description: A federal appeals court found Trump’s declaration of an “invasion” at the U.S.-Mexico border was illegal, effectively reopening the U.S. to migrants seeking asylum.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Are we ableist for not inviting relative with anxiety around food?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Are we ableist for not inviting relative with anxiety around food?

item.title: Justice Dept. drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: Officials had been investigating cost overruns connected to a large Fed office renovation. Powell has denied any wrongdoing.

item.title: The costs of Trump’s contempt are starting to show
item.description: The costs of Trump’s contempt are starting to show

item.title: Netanyahu reveals cancer diagnosis, says he hid it from public because of Iran war
item.description: The Israeli prime minister said he delayed revealing his diagnosis for two months to prevent Iran from using it as “propaganda.” He said treatment had left “no trace” of the cancer.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.

item.title: Hezbollah defiant in face of Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension
item.description: Hezbollah called the three-week ceasefire extension announced by President Donald Trump “meaningless,” as Israel carried out fresh strikes in southern Lebanon on Friday.

item.title: Middle East. Putin invite. Prediction markets. Elon Musk. NFL draft. Attention spans. Gargantuan octopus. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: 4 science-backed steps for forgiving those who’ve wronged you (even if they don’t deserve it)
item.description: Some scientists say training people to practice forgiveness could be a powerful public health tool.

item.title: Meet Carter Faith, the breakout country star who’s not afraid to speak up
item.description: The singer, whose honky-tonk bangers are both witty and heartbreaking, is bantering with Glen Powell, blowing off the Kid Rock tour and selling out venues — without radio airplay.

item.title: Ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will be extended for three weeks, Trump says
item.description: The ceasefire was due to expire Sunday. The ambassador-level negotiations extended the fragile peace, but it remains unclear whether the Lebanese government can keep Hezbollah in check.

item.title: U.S. soldier accused of using inside information about Maduro’s capture to win $400,000 on prediction market
item.description: Prosecutors accused Gannon Ken Van Dyke, an active duty soldier involved in the planning and execution of the U.S. operation, of using his access to classified information to place a series of Polymarket bets on Nicolás Maduro’s future and whether U.S. forces would enter Venezuela. The case against Van Dyke is believed to be the first time the Justice Department has prosecuted an insider trading case based on prediction market bets.

item.title: Pentagon fires ombudsman overseeing Stars and Stripes, months after calling the military newspaper ‘woke’
item.description: Jacqueline Smith said the Defense Department fired her without giving a reason, according to an email reviewed by The Post. However, Smith said she believed she was fired for speaking out against the decision to overhaul Stars and Stripes, announced by chief Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell in January, a decision she said threatened the paper’s long-held independence.

item.title: I saw Bob Dylan play. You can, too.
item.description: Bob Dylan will come to you.

item.title: Third U.S. aircraft carrier arrives in waters near Iran
item.description: The path of the USS George H.W. Bush strike group, which includes the carrier and the warships escorting it, to the Middle East has been closely watched as President Donald Trump demands progress in peace negotiations with Tehran.

item.title: DOJ watchdog launches review of agency’s compliance with Epstein files law
item.description: The audit by the department’s inspector general follows months of lawmaker complaints over the congressionally mandated release of investigative files.

item.title: ‘FBoy Island’ contestant’s bet on his own decision to run for Senate exposes perils of prediction platform Kalshi
item.description: A former reality TV contestant turned candidate in Virginia and two U.S. House hopefuls in Texas and Minnesota have been banned from the prediction market for five years.

item.title: Trump ally asked FIFA to have Italy replace Iran at World Cup
item.description: Italian officials expressed no interest in a substitution that would give Italy’s national team a charitable berth after failing to qualify for the tournament.

item.title: Trump doesn’t need a deal to get what he wants from Iran
item.description: The regime is running out of oil storage, money — and time.

item.title: A gubernatorial candidate in today’s On the Record
item.description: A gubernatorial candidate in today’s On the Record

item.title: Trump administration reclassifies medical marijuana as lower-risk drug
item.description: The Justice Department said the drug is now downgraded to Schedule III, similar to some common prescription painkillers. It said the change would boost medical research.

item.title: The unflattering secrets revealed so far in Elon Musk’s latest legal feud
item.description: Hundreds of court filings have unearthed cringey texts, emails or private diary entries of Musk, Sam Altman, other OpenAI founders and more public figures.

item.title: Gas prices. Pete Hegseth. Redistricting fight. Marijuana. Billionaire secrets. Meditation benefits. Pants fashion. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Don’t panic about summer flight prices yet. Here’s what to do.
item.description: The war in Iran has created a sense of urgency in trip planning, a race against rising airfares and vanishing routes. To help travelers navigate this fraught landscape, we compiled the most critical information for summer vacation planning.

item.title: John Phelan, who officials said repeatedly clashed with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, is out as Navy secretary
item.description: Phelan, a billionaire and significant contributor to President Donald Trump’s campaign, is exiting abruptly after 13 tumultuous months at the Pentagon. Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become the service’s acting secretary, a spokesman for Hegseth said.

item.title: CDC won’t publish report showing that covid shots cut likelihood of hospital visits
item.description: A report showing the efficacy of coronavirus shots that was previously delayed by the head of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has been blocked from being published in the agency’s flagship scientific journal, according to three people familiar with the decision. The move has raised concerns among current and former officials that information about the vaccine’s benefits is being downplayed because it conflicts with the views of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

item.title: The 2026 blue wave might be smaller than you think
item.description: The 2026 blue wave might be smaller than you think

item.title: Trump is considering an Iran deal with many of the trade-offs he blasted Obama for accepting
item.description: As U.S. envoys pursue peace talks, the president confronts persistent sticking points: sanctions relief, enrichment limits and the risk of empowering Tehran.

item.title: Israel says it will open direct talks with Lebanon but not halt attacks on Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that his country was ready for direct negotiations amid international calls for Israel to stop its bombing in Lebanon.

item.title: Emperor penguins have just been declared endangered
item.description: The Antarctic fur seal also was newly labeled as endangered by a key international group, which cited a warming world.

item.title: Countless Americans face a scary retirement. This rule could change that.
item.description: When it comes to retirement investing, the deck is stacked against most workers.

item.title: A statue for this animal in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: The Supreme Court remade by Trump has ushered in historic defeats for civil rights
item.description: The court is the first since at least the ’50s to reject claims in a majority of cases involving women and minorities, an analysis conducted for The Post shows.

item.title: Iran ceasefire. Covid vaccines. Pam Bondi. Gilgo Beach suspect. Supreme Court. Social media detox. Artemis II. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: The Iran war’s exit ramp has become a diplomatic maze
item.description: The war began without a strategy for victory. The peace has no formula either.

item.title: 7 walkable U.S. cities where you don’t need a car to have a great vacation
item.description: These fun, picturesque urban centers invite visitors to explore on foot.

item.title: Iran accuses U.S. and Israel of ceasefire violations, as traffic remains stuck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran might withdraw from talks if violations of the ceasefire continued, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a statement Wednesday, which detailed apparent breaches of the agreement, including Israeli attacks in Lebanon, a drone intercepted in Iran and what he said was President Donald Trump’s denial of Iran’s right to enrich uranium.

item.title: A new study shows simple ways to avoid dementia
item.description: Keeping mentally engaged can reduce dementia risk, especially among older adults.

item.title: As you age, eating these 7 nutrients becomes even more important
item.description: There are certain vitamins, minerals and macronutrients that everyone needs to pay more attention to after they reach their 50s and 60s.

item.title: ‘Ketamine Queen’ sentenced to 15 years over Matthew Perry’s death
item.description: Jasveen Sangha pleaded guilty in September to drug-related charges, including providing the actor with the ketamine that killed him.

item.title: Pam Bondi won’t testify before House about Epstein files, Department of Justice says
item.description: Some House members say they still intend to pursue their subpoena of the former attorney general, despite her firing last week.

item.title: I’m an American living in Europe. It’s leaving the U.S. — fast.
item.description: Europeans are hedging against coercion in security, trade, education and everyday life.

item.title: They go halfsies, but, surprise! Husband has millions. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Her mom taught her to insist on maintaining separate finances. So her husband, separately, got himself very rich.

item.title: If you aren’t losing weight with GLP-1 drugs, this may be one reason why
item.description: Two genes involved in gut hormones that influence appetite and digestion may be a factor in how people respond to the weight loss drugs, according to a new study.

item.title: How do restaurants decide which unlucky diner gets stuck next to the bathroom? Our food critic is answering questions.
item.description: Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Trump’s apocalyptic Iran threat paused war but fueled debate about his credibility, morality
item.description: The president’s threat to wipe out “a whole civilization” raised the possibility that he would go even beyond his earlier warning to attack civilian infrastructure, which violates the laws of war. His post set off nuclear panic and drew condemnation across the political spectrum.

item.title: Iran ceasefire. Pete Hegseth. Election results. Artemis photos. Kidnapped journalist. Rising prices. Hero rat. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.
